---
title: "Baking & Entity References"
---

Baking remaps the `Entity` references it can see. The rule that prevents the
hardest class of bug is knowing exactly which references it can see and which it
cannot.

## What gets remapped

| Container | Entity fields remapped? |
|---|---|
| {{< unity-type IComponentData >}} with `Entity` fields | yes |
| `DynamicBuffer<`{{< unity-type IBufferElementData >}}`>` with `Entity` fields | yes |
| `BlobAssetReference` with `Entity` fields | no |
| {{< unity-type IDynamicHashMap >}}`<K, Entity>` | no |

::: {.axiom title="Remapping follows reflection"}
The TypeManager remaps `Entity` fields it can find by reflection. Entities
buried in opaque byte storage — blobs, dynamic hash map values — are invisible
to it and survive baking as stale indices.
:::

## The dynamic hash map trap

`IDynamicHashMap<ushort, Entity>` stores its entity values in opaque buffer
bytes. After baking, every value is a stale baking-time index. The symptom is
precise and recognizable.

::: {.failure title="TryGetValue true, Exists false, Version 1"}
`TryGetValue` returns `true`, but `EntityManager.Exists` on the returned entity
is `false`, and every stale entity reports `Version=1`. That is unremapped
entity storage, not a logic bug.
:::

```{.exec name="Detect stale entity references" usings="Unity.Entities,BovineLabs.Core.Iterators"}
var em = World.DefaultGameObjectInjectionWorld.EntityManager;
var q = em.CreateEntityQuery(typeof(EntityLinkSource));
var entities = q.ToEntityArray(Unity.Collections.Allocator.Temp);
var report = "no links found";
var i = 0;
while (i < entities.Length) {
    if (em.HasBuffer<EntityLink>(entities[i])) {
        var map = em.GetBuffer<EntityLink>(entities[i]).AsHashMap<EntityLink, ushort, Entity>();
        if (map.TryGetValue(0, out var target)) {
            report = $"key 0 -> exists={em.Exists(target)} version={target.Version}";
            break;
        }
    }
    i++;
}
entities.Dispose();
return report;
```

## The three fixes

The fixes trade complexity for visibility. Prefer the one that makes the entity
references visible to remapping rather than the one that patches around the
invisibility.

1. **Baking system re-resolve.** A `BakingSystem` that runs after all bakers
   re-resolves the entities and writes valid references back into the map.
2. **Companion buffer.** Keep a `DynamicBuffer<T>` with explicit `Entity` fields
   alongside the map. The buffer is remapped automatically; rebuild the map from
   it at runtime.
3. **Replace the map.** Use a plain `DynamicBuffer<T : IBufferElementData>` with
   `ushort Key; Entity Value;` fields. Lookup is O(n), but remapping works
   because the fields are visible.

## After a baking fix, the baker must re-run

A baking change is not verified until the baker re-runs, which means stopping and
re-entering play mode. Then verify the references resolve.

```{.cli name="Re-bake and verify"}
unity-cli editor stop
unity-cli editor refresh --force --compile
unity-cli console --clear
unity-cli editor play
echo 'var em = World.DefaultGameObjectInjectionWorld.EntityManager; var q = em.CreateEntityQuery(typeof(BovineLabs.Timeline.EntityLinks.Data.EntityLinkSource)); return q.CalculateEntityCount();' | unity-cli exec
```
